# Accessibility Audit — checkout flow

## Executive summary

A mock report from a *different* suite auditor, emitted against the same shared contract
to prove portability. Two issues: a contrast failure and an unlabelled control.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| 4 — Catastrophe | 1 |
| 3 — Major | 0 |
| 2 — Minor | 1 |
| 1 — Cosmetic | 0 |

## Prioritized fixes

1. [sev4] "Place order" button text fails contrast
2. [sev2] Promo-code input has no programmatic label

## Findings

### [sev4] "Place order" button text fails contrast

- **Issue Description:** White text on the lime button renders at 1.9:1, well below the
  4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
- **Framework Violation:** WCAG 2.2 — 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum), Level AA.
- **Severity:** 4 — catastrophe; the primary action is effectively unreadable.
- **Evidence:** live capture `./assets/place-order-contrast.png`; computed on the render.
- **Recommended Fix:** darken the button or use `on-primary` text meeting 4.5:1.

### [sev2] Promo-code input has no programmatic label

- **Issue Description:** The promo field has placeholder text but no `<label>` or
  `aria-label`, so screen readers announce it as an unlabelled edit field.
- **Framework Violation:** WCAG 2.2 — 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, Level A.
- **Severity:** 2 — minor; usable with effort but not conveyed to assistive tech.
- **Evidence:** live a11y tree at `/checkout`; `src/checkout/PromoField.tsx:88`.
- **Recommended Fix:** add a visible or visually-hidden `<label for>` / `aria-label`.

## Appendix

- **Mode:** live.
- **Frameworks applied:** wcag-2.2.
- **Coverage / not inspected:** guest-checkout path (auth-gated) — not audited.
